title: [reduce] Add a first proof-of-concept reducer implementation with sample FIRRTL dialect reducers
body:
Add a first implementation of `circt-reduce` that actually performs some useful reduction. It is inspired by what is being done upstream with `mlir-reduce`, but uses a more specific implementation that targets what is immediately useful for us. As upstream evolves (and maybe we can help with that), we'll be able to switch over to the `mlir-reduce` framework again.

This PR also adds a handful of sample reduction patterns mainly operating on the FIRRTL dialect to have a concrete example of how things can reduce. This allowed me to reduce a >200M FIRRTL test case to a 2.7K test case (essentially just a bunch of wires and assigns). This is not complete at all, and down the road we'll want to gather the reduction patterns from the dialects directly rather than hacking them together in `circt-reduce.cpp`, but this works as a first useful implementation of the tool.

Usage is as follows:
```
circt-reduce <input.mlir> --test <path-to-test-script> -o <output-file>
```
And optionally a `--keep-best` flag to write to the output file whenever a new smallest reduction is found, rather than just writing the result at the very end. This is useful for observing the progress of the tool, or debugging tool crashes.
